2603.22765.title: DALDALL: Data Augmentation for Lexical and Semantic Diverse in Legal Domain by leveraging LLM-Persona
2603.22765.paper_summary: Data scarcity remains a persistent challenge in low-resource domains. While existing data augmentation methods leverage the generative capabilities of large language models (LLMs) to produce large volumes of synthetic data, these approaches often prioritize quantity over quality and lack domain-specific strategies. In this work, we introduce DALDALL, a persona-based data augmentation framework tailored for legal information retrieval (IR). Our method employs domain-specific professional personas--such as attorneys, prosecutors, and judges--to generate synthetic queries that exhibit substantially greater lexical and semantic diversity than vanilla prompting approaches. Experiments on the CLERC and COLIEE benchmarks demonstrate that persona-based augmentation achieves improvement in lexical diversity as measured by Self-BLEU scores, while preserving semantic fidelity to the original queries. Furthermore, dense retrievers fine-tuned on persona-augmented data consistently achieve competitive or superior recall performance compared to those trained on original data or generic augmentations. These findings establish persona-based prompting as an effective strategy for generating high-quality training data in specialized, low-resource domains.
2603.22765.paper_summary_zh: 數據稀缺仍然是低資源領域的一個持續挑戰。雖然現有的數據增強方法利用大型語言模型（LLMs）的生成能力來產生大量的合成數據，但這些方法往往優先考慮數量而非質量，並且缺乏特定領域的策略。在這項工作中，我們介紹了DALDALL，一個針對法律信息檢索（IR）量身定制的基於角色的數據增強框架。我們的方法利用特定領域的專業角色——例如律師、檢察官和法官——來生成合成查詢，這些查詢在詞彙和語義多樣性上顯著超過普通提示方法。在CLERC和COLIEE基準上的實驗表明，基於角色的增強在自我BLEU分數測量的詞彙多樣性上取得了改善，同時保持了對原始查詢的語義忠實。此外，基於角色增強數據進行微調的密集檢索器在召回性能上始終達到與訓練於原始數據或通用增強相比的競爭或更優的表現。這些發現確立了基於角色的提示作為在專業低資源領域生成高質量訓練數據的有效策略。
2603.22765.author: Janghyeok Choi et.al.
2603.22765.authors: Janghyeok Choi, Jaewon Lee, Sungzoon Cho
